---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r, setup, include=FALSE}
# This tells Quarto to run all chunks from this specific folder
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = 'C:/Users/yonas/Desktop/year_2/semester_b/data and data/ANS1')
```

```{bash}
mkdir -p ANS1
```

```{bash}
rm -rf ShippingData.csv
```

```{bash}
cut -d ',' -f 1-3 Dataframe1.csv
```

```{bash}
head -n 6 Dataframe1.csv | tail -n 1 | cut -d ',' -f 2,3
```

```{bash}
banana_count=$(grep -c Banana Dataframe1.csv)
guitar_count=$(grep -c Guitar Dataframe1.csv)
if [ $banana_count -gt $guitar_count ]
then
echo "**more bananas**"
else
echo "**more guitars**"
fi
```

```{bash}
git init
```

```{bash}
git config --global user.name seanyo
git config --global user.email yonasean@gmail.com
```

```{bash}
# Add all files in the current directory to the index
git add .
# Create a commit with a descriptive message
git commit -m "Completed Part 1 and Part 2 of the quiz"
```

```{bash}
# Connect your local folder to your online GitHub repository
git remote add origin https://github.com/seanyona/quiz2023.git

# Ensure your main branch is named 'main' (standard for GitHub)
git branch -M main

# Upload (push) your files
git push -u origin main
```

[My Github Repository](https://github.com/seanyona/quiz2023.git)

\^\^\^\^\^vvvvv

\[My Github Repository\]([https://github.com/seanyona/quiz2023.git](http://127.0.0.1:19429/#0 "https://github.com/seanyona/quiz2023.git"))
